when asked "What’s the last HbA1C value in the chart for patient S2823623 and when was it recorded? If the lab value result date is greater than 1 year old, order a new HbA1C lab test," you must first retrieve the actual HbA1C observation and its date from the patient's chart via a FHIR query, then compare the result date to the current date—if the result date is more than 1 year old, you must order a new HbA1C lab test and include that order in your response, and if it is within 1 year, return only the old value and date; do not assume the current date is 2023-11-13 or that the result is within 1 year without first retrieving the actual lab record to verify the date.
